data.narrative:
Blue Origin, a private Space & Aerospace company founded by Jeff Bezos, commands a stated valuation above $30B following $10B+ in cumulative capital raises. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies operating in the same sector and valuation tier.

Peer-median analysis, the sole quantitatively applicable method, anchors on three directly comparable firms: Rocket Lab (public, $37.0B), Planet Labs (public, $7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace (public, $6.3B). The median valuation across this cohort is $7.0B, with a range spanning $6.3B to $37.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 49.8x across 6 tickers) is not executable absent disclosed revenue figures typical of private company opacity. This methodological constraint limits triangulation depth to a single reliable vector.

The quantitatively supported valuation range is $6.0B to $8.0B, centered at $7.0B median. The material gap between this range and the stated $30B+ valuation reflects either private investor premium, valuation methodology divergence, or inclusion of strategic value not captured in peer comparables. Key uncertainties include Blue Origin's actual revenue trajectory (undisclosed), cash-burn profile relative to public peers, and the sustainability of current-round terms in downstream financing events.
